name: Replicate contacts across systems with HubSpot and Google Drive 24/7

parameters.text: =✅ Replicate contacts across systems with HubSpot and Google Drive 24/7 — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 7:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Map to Canonical Schema

### Map to Canonical Schema

Shapes the item: writes `recordId`, `kind`, `payload`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Route by Event Kind

### Route by Event Kind

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: created, updated, deleted + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Handle 'created' → HubSpot · ②→ Handle 'updated' → HubSpot · ③→ Handle 'deleted' → HubSpot · ④→ Log Unrecognized Kind

### Handle 'created' → HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Consolidate Handling · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Handle 'updated' → HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Consolidate Handling · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Handle 'deleted' → HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Consolidate Handling · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Log Unrecognized Kind

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Consolidate Handling · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Consolidate Handling

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per record (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Record Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Copy to Google Drive

### Archive Copy to Google Drive

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Summarize Run Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Archive Copy to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (HubSpot)

### Append Audit Row (HubSpot)

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#data-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#data-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Record Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Replicate contacts across systems with HubSpot and Google Drive 24/7

**Pattern:** Two-System Sync · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Replicate contacts across systems with HubSpot and Google Drive 24/7. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual etl is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Map to Canonical Schema → Route by Event Kind → Handle 'created' → HubSpot → Consolidate Handling → Fingerprint for Idempotency → Drop Already-Processed Items → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to two-system sync without manual effort
- Anyone automating etl in data
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Scales without extra headcount
- Improves response time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; HubSpot private-app token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The optional HMAC check runs before any business logic touches the payload — bad signatures are rejected at the door rather than after work has already been done with untrusted data. Acking fast (HTTP 202) and doing the real work after the response is what stops the sender's webhook delivery from timing out and re-delivering the same event.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Turn on signature verification and trace what a tampered payload does at the 'Signature Valid?' gate.
2. Add a new event kind to the switch and give it its own handler.
3. Remove the fast-ack step and observe how response time changes under a slow downstream write.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your etl process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
